title: Cynanchum adriaticum
taxonomy: Cynanchum adriaticum is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes various species of plants that are often characterized by their milky sap. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum adriaticum is also part of the order Gentianales. Within the genus Cynanchum, Cynanchum adriaticum is further classified as a species of flowering plant. The genus Cynanchum itself is a group of plants that are often known as the Vincetoxicum or dog vine.

intro: Cynanchum adriaticum, also known as Adriatic Cynanchum or Black Hellebore, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its dark green, lance-shaped leaves and small, white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its purported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum adriaticum include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular-protective effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create syrups, teas, and infusions for the treatment of various health conditions.
